tokens: Tony, we're here, and I'm getting off; and then I'll read you the readings when I can brush it off so I can see.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: This thing is covered with dirt.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: And our LCRU is heating up, so we'd like a good (dusting) job on those.
Even though we know you do it every time.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: The plan here is a rake soil first, together; and after you've done that, we'd like John to take an
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Let me get a pan first.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Charlie, how does this look for the antenna pointing?
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Looks about right on to me.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: But that's hard...What's the signal strength?
(Burst of static as John turns on the TV)
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: That pattern on the ground (is) the mirror reflecting.
I thought we'd really found something, Tony!
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Hey, we('ve) got a picture again.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: We had a little trouble pointing the antenna.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Okay, Tony, this area here is on a...We're on about a 5-degree slope away from North Ray, and this big block that you'll see in a moment is downslope, filleted predominantly downslope here.
The surrounding terrain is covered with - not covered but 10 percent (covered) - with cobbles (about 64 mm to 256 mm).
It's very subdued on the meter-sized craters.
In fact, it's a very smooth plain, but on a slope.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: The rock types here appear to be the same as we sampled up on top (at the North Ray rim), but we'll get you a rake-soil (sample) out in front of this big boulder over here.
(To John and/or himself) Let's see; we need to get the bags and stuff.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Okay, John, both of us got bags (meaning SCBs) on our backs, so why don't we just take these little bags.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: I'm getting tongs for a gnomon.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Okay, Charlie; if you could grab the bottom of the gnomon in the sheath there, we could use that for our color and photometric scale.
We won't have the level (means "vertical"), but at least we'll get part of it.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Well, you could; (joking) if there wasn't so much igneous soil on it.
When I have it, we'll put it out there.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: (Joining in the laughter) I can't believe it.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: See those - about four or five - little rocks right there?
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Stick her down right there and let me...I'm sort of turned around here on my direction.
I think I'm facing...South is over this way.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Up the slope, adroitly; like a gazelle!
Got to Station 13, Tony, and it sure looks good.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: (Let me) get a bag from you, Charlie.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: There's some glass in there, a black chip.
In one rake, we got about 10 little...And the regolith here, Tony, seems to be a little bit more loosely compacted than up on the top (meaning on the rim of North Ray).
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: I can't get my gnomon (meaning the tongs) in.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: (Pause) (To Tony) Not very productive, though, on the small chips.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: ...if I'd rake upslope, though, I bet you I'd get more.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: There's about 20 small rocks going into bag 343.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: (I've got to) get another bag from you, Charlie.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Ah, the old suit, (it) wins every time.
(Pause) I can't bend this beauty like we could on the training grounds.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: (Garbled) Okay, that looks like 2 scoopsfuls (sic) going into bag 346.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: If it gets out of there, we'll call it Houdini.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: And Charlie, you might look around...
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Houston, the big eye (meaning the TV) is looking right at that big rock.
What do you think of that beauty?
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: That's exactly what we're looking at...
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: ...While John's doing the LPM, we'd like you to hammer on that rock a bit.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: That's what I had in mind.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: (On his own initiative) And if you get a chance - and it looks like some soil right on the south side, kind of underneath, might be permanently shadowed - you might take some of those and just put it in a bag.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Well, Houston, I didn't park too good to do the LPM.
If I go 45 feet from here, I'm going to be in the middle of a crater.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: You don't mind if I go out behind the Rover, for example?
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: (Answering John just before Experiments tells him the same thing) No, that's fine.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Instead of at right angles to it.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: John, where's those bags that's still got the bracket on it.
Is it under your seat here?
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: The bags that had the brackets on them?
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: I think they were about out.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Okay, if you go out south, be a little careful on that cable; it'll be pulling at 90 degrees then.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Okay, well I think I'll go out as far...I'll go out south...(Pause) How about east-southeast?
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: It's off to the starboard bow or something.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Tony, we're about out of bags!
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Oh, you've been really packing them away.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Yeah, we've only lost one set of (unused) bags, Charlie.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Well, we've got a few left here.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: I'm going to get on the sunset...(Correcting himself) sunlit side, Tony, so I'll know what (pause) I'm whacking on here.
That might be a permanently-shadowed soil right in there.
I think it is, as a matter of fact.
It'll pass (meaning it will be suitable).
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Good show, let's get one of those.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: I sure wish we had the S(ESC)...(Stops to listen) Okay, I'll do it.
(To Tony) Hey, what's the (Hasselblad) settings for in there (into the deep shadow)?
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: That is a perfect shadowed soil sample.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: John, you couldn't have picked a better rock.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: (Pause) I have to get my (gold) visor up to see something.
(Laughing) Man, I can't believe I'm going (to reach) under this beauty.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Well, I don't know how long that rock's been there, but that dirt has been shadowed ever since it's been there.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: That's way...I got it from about a meter up under there, Tony.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: And I'm sorry, but it's gonna have to go in an old plastic bag, here, (instead of the SESC).
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Any chance of getting soil underneath that now for the control?
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Underneath where you just took (the first sample); just dig deeper.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: That thing (meaning the Sun) is bright.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: (Responding to Tony's request for a deep soil sample) Yeah, I can get that for you.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Ah, I forgot to turn that thing on before I left the Rover.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: (Subvocal) I don't know how to turn it on.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Okay, how long does it take it to warm up, Tony?
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: We'll take a mark now, and go a minute and a half, and I'll tell you when (to start the measurement).
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: That would have...John, that shopping bag would have been...Should have gone down to the supermarket and bought one.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: They give 'em to you at the supermarket, Charlie.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: (To Houston) See how that sample bag is...
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: ...is sitting up there - (correcting himself) I mean that SCB - is sitting up there, Tony.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: What you need, looks like to me, is a bag that has two handles on it...
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Let me put that in the bag.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: That ain't very much, but we'll keep ...
Oh, you just kicked (some soil on the sample area)...Well, I got enough.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: That'll be good on the soil sample.
And we'd like to spend the rest of the time - and there isn't much of it - hammering on that rock.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Okay, there's about 50 grams in the control (soil sample).
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: It just might be permanently shadowed, Houston; because it's downslope, and when the...Golly.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: I think it...John, I reached back in there about...Pull your visor up and look under there.
I reached in there about 2 to 3 feet, it looked like to me.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Oh, that there is one of those gopher holes.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: (do) you think you can push that one over?
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Do that (meaning reach under a rock) in West Texas and you...(Stops to listen) No.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: (Finishing the thought) You do that in West Texas, and you get a rattlesnake.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: One thing about this rock is it has some...This is the one that I noticed when we were coming up the way (to Station 11) that had some of these holes in it (that) look like vesicles, Charlie.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Yep, they sure do, big ones.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: I'm out of film, I think.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: We'd like magazine Foxtrot (Apollo magazine 117) on your camera.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: And, John, you can do the LPM any time (because it has warmed up).
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: What a time to run out of (film)...Okay...(Stops to listen to Tony)
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Let me take a picture for you, Charlie.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Tony, this is the black matrix with some excellent crystals in it that also are milky in color.
Don't see any cleavage though or striations.
(The crystals are) about a centimeter across; and it has a matrix (means "clasts") of that white rock, like up on the rim.
(Correcting himself) Not a matrix, but some clasts of that (white material).
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: You'll need magazine Foxtrot, and we're about out of time here...
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Okay, Houston, we're ready to take the L(PM or Lunar Portable Magnetometer)...(Stops to listen)
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: We copied that, John; and (please put) visor down.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: (Stops to listen) I didn't put it up.
Read Switch, Off; Power Switch is Off.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Just get a couple of samples there, and you should be about ready to go then when John gets that (magnetometer cable) reeled up.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Okay, I got a couple...a handful of chips there.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: When you get under the dirt, it's all white.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: (To himself) Great place to pick it.
Two great places to whack it.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Well, I didn't want to fall down...Now I got it.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: I got three chips off the rock, (which were originally) scattered over about a 2-meter area (of the rock surface).
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: One of them is too big to go in the bags, but the other ones, right now, are going in 429.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: And we'd like you to go back (to the Rover) and start loading up.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: (Pause) (I've got to) get this other rock.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: That zee-ing...(correcting himself) That's swinging; it really works.
John's got a long way to go, Tony, before he gets that thing reeled in.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Thanks for those words of encouragement.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: And, Tony, this rock here looks like...It's the same character as the ones (like House Rock) up on the rim (of North Ray).
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: (It's like) that great, huge black one that we sampled, except that one up there didn't have any of these holes in it.
I can't really say what these holes are here.
What caused them, I don't know.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: They look more vuggy to me; although they're round.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: They look like drill holes is what they look like.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: They look like...You know what they look like?
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: ...they look like those holes you get in rocks where the...
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Here, put those (tools) up for me.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: They look like the holes that you get in rocks where you have a venting of gas that comes up through there like a long...You know what I mean, Tony?
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Let me put the scoop back.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: (Lost in the switchover from the high-gain to the low gain)...(I'm turning the TV fully) counter-clockwise.
That (TV) is a beautiful little piece of gear.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Charlie, you've got about 4 minutes (of film left) on the DAC...
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: ...at 12 frames per second; and you can either use it that way or one frame per second, either way you want, on the way back, whichever looks best to you.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Why don't we go at one frame a second all the way back in, to the Rover (means the LM)?
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: What am I hung up on, John?
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Your bag is hung up, Charlie.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: You know, that's probably why yours came off.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Yeah, you got to...(Pause) There you go.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: And, we'd like your frame (count)s before you load up.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: I'm (frame) 6 on magazine Foxtrot, and I finished up...
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: That Smoky Mountain is a steep-sided mountain, Tony.
I've got a good view of Ravine here, and it's steep sided on the Smoky Mountain side (meaning the north wall and rim), but very undulating on the other side.
On the Cayley side (meaning the south rim).
And you can see Cat Crater, and it doesn't look very blocky, so I guess it's probably...It's sharper than the rest, but it's still no blocks around it.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: I didn't shut off the TV.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Charlie, if you haven't gotten on yet, we'd like to change that to 12 frames.
Evidently, your DAC is about out of electrical power.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: I'll start it when we get started, but it's on 12.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: This has been a good traverse up here...
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: It sure looked good down here, I'll tell you.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: I'm sure glad we got this EVA-3...
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Hope we picked up the right rocks.
I think there are two predominant types.
But there's two predominant-type rocks here, the aphanitic black-looking ones that really appear to be crystalline to me, and not necessarily lava like.
transcript.speaker: Duke
